title: Developmental Abnormalities of the Genitourinary System
authors: Courtney K. Rowe & Paul A. Merguerian

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Genitourinary Developmental Abnormalities?
CAKUTs (คือ congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract) พบใน 0.5% ของ pregnancies และคิดเป็น 20–30% ของ prenatally diagnosed anomalies ทั้งหมด. สำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) CAKUTs เป็นสาเหตุ 2/3 ของ childhood CKD; (2) Posterior urethral valves (PUV) = bladder outlet obstruction ที่พบบ่อยที่สุดใน newborn males — nadir creatinine > 1.0 mg/dL บ่งบอก ESRD; (3) bilateral nonpalpable testicles = evaluate for CAH/DSD ก่อนเสมอ — อย่ากลับบ้านโดยไม่ให้เสียโอกาส; (4) VCUG indication: high-grade hydronephrosis, bilateral hydronephrosis, หรือ แรกหลัง UTI สำหรับ หาก ultrasound ผิดปกติ; (5) Bladder exstrophy: repair delay to 3 months — ไม่ต้องผ่าตั้งแต่ birth; (6) Prune-belly syndrome = triad: absent abdominal wall + cryptorchidism + urinary tract dilatation, 95% male, 40% จะเป็น CKD.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย embryology ของ kidney development: pronephros → mesonephros → metanephros; ureteric bud; nephron formation เสร็จที่ 34 weeks
รู้ renal agenesis: bilateral vs unilateral, Potter sequence, OHVIRA syndrome
อธิบาย cystic kidney diseases: ARPKD (PKHD1), ADPKD (PKD1/PKD2), MCDK — แยกกันด้วย genetics, presentation, prognosis
รู้ neonatal renal tumors: congenital mesoblastic nephroma vs Wilms tumor — ความถี่ สถิติ survival, syndromes
วินิจฉัย UPJO, megaureter, VUR, ureterocele: mechanism, grading, indications สำหรับ VCUG
PUV: only in males, keyhole sign, VCUG confirmation, nadir Cr นัยเชิง ESRD; vesicostomy vs valve ablation
Bladder exstrophy/cloacal exstrophy/OEIS complex: prenatal นี้หมายถึงอะไร, immediate newborn management, repair timing (3 months)
Cryptorchidism: ความชุก term vs preterm, AUA guideline (refer 6 months), orchiopexy 6–18 months; bilateral = นึกถึง DSD/CAH
Hypospadias/chordee: 1:250–300 males, GMS scoring, surgery 6–18 months; เมื่อไหร่ต้อง evaluate DSD
รู้ female genital anomalies: hydrocolpos, MRKH (1:5000), cloacal anomaly (1:35,000), Mullerian duplication, introital masses DDx
Prune-belly, myelodysplasia, VACTERL: triad/presentation, renal surveillance protocol, urologic goals
1. Embryology ของ Kidney and Urinary Tract
1.1 Three Kidney Stages
Pronephros: nonfunctional tubules → หายไปสิ้น week 4; ติดต่อเป็น nephric duct (ND)

Mesonephros: vascularized glomeruli + proximal/distal tubules; Mesonephric duct (Wolffian duct) อยู่จน puberty ใน male → เป็น vas deferens, seminal vesicles, epididymis, efferent ductules; ใน female → vestigial epoophoron + paroophoron
Metanephros: final kidney; starts 5–6 weeks; ureteric bud ออกจาก caudal mesonephric duct → penetrates metanephric blastema → branching เป็น collecting system
Nephron formation: mesenchymal-to-epithelial transition ที่ ureteric bud tips → nephrons. All nephrons formed by 34 weeks; adult humans: 210,000–2.7 million nephrons per kidney
Ureteral recanalization: obliteration แล้ว recanalize — incomplete → UPJO หรือ ureterovesical junction obstruction
Vesicoureteral reflux origin: ectopic budding จาก Wolffian duct → lateral displacement → short intramural tunnel → VUR

สรุป: Embryology → CAKUT Pathophysiology
ทำไม CAKUT ถึงสำคัญ? เพราะ defect ใน same molecular pathway — ureteric bud branching + metanephric mesenchyme induction. ถ้า bud ออกผิดตำแหน่ง → ectopic ureter; ถ้า recanalization ไม่สมบูรณ์ → UPJO; ถ้า bud อยู่สูงกว่าปกติ → ectopic ureter; ถ้าโต ถ้ามี branching น้อย → renal hypoplasia/dysplasia. Genitourinary Molecular Anatomy Project ระบุว่ามี several hundred genes ที่เกี่ยวข้อง.
2. Anomalies of the Kidney
2.1 Renal Agenesis

rows: Severe oligohydramnios; no bladder fill; absent renal arteries on Doppler
Usually asymptomatic; empty renal fossa on US (47% absent kidney, rest = ectopia)
Potter facies: skin fold eye→cheek, blunted nose, low-set ears, club legs; pulmonary hypoplasia → respiratory distress; no urine in 24 hr
Almost universally fatal: 40% in-utero death; born alive < 48 hr (pulmonary compromise)
50% need dialysis by age 30; higher risk ESRD than donor nephrectomy
Serial amnioinfusion (experimental); peritoneal dialysis attempted in selected cases; prognosis remains poor
Monitor BP, urine protein, renal function; sports: AAP allows non-contact

blocks.text:
OHVIRA Syndrome — อย่าลืมตรวจใน unilateral renal agenesis หญิง
Unilateral renal agenesis ใน females → สัมพันธ์กับ OHVIRA: Obstructed Hemivagina with Ipsilateral Renal Agenesis — จะแสดงอาการที่ menarche จาก retained menstrual blood ใน obstructed hemivagina. ดังนั้น ระอู screening ที่ puberty. นอกจากนี้ยังมี risk ของ contralateral renal anomaly (UPJO, VUR) ด้วย.
2.2 Renal Ectopia and Fusion Anomalies

Renal ectopia: kidney fails to ascend to standard location; incidence 1 in 700; pelvic, iliac, abdominal, thoracic, crossed; 50% hydronephrotic; strong association with extrarenal anomalies (เกือบ 50%)
Crossed renal ectopia: 7.5 per 10,000 newborns; 90% fused; 20–40% have VUR; ผู้ชาย > ผู้หญิง; left-to-right crossover บ่อยที่สุด
Horseshoe kidney: 1 in 600 บน autopsy; male predominance; สัมพันธ์ Edwards/Down/Turner syndrome; VUR, UPJO ใน 50%; Wilms tumor ชุกขึ้น; ~1/3 develop stones in adulthood
2.3 Cystic Kidney Diseases

rows: Bilateral hyperechoic kidneys, poor CMD, oligohydramnios, Potter facies, respiratory distress
20% die of pulmonary complications; 50% need early renal replacement; liver/kidney transplant
Usually asymptomatic in infancy; early detection on prenatal US; 20% children: hypertension/proteinuria
Renal failure typically adulthood; avoid routine ultrasound/cyst counting in asymptomatic children
Bunch-of-grapes cysts on US; no renal parenchyma; poor renal function
60% involute spontaneously; early nephrectomy rarely needed; contralateral VUR: VCUG if contralateral anomaly
Hypopigmented skin lesions, epilepsy; renal: AML + cysts + oncocytoma
mTOR inhibitor everolimus; US every 1–3 yr; surgery for emergent AML hemorrhage

blocks.text: 2.4 Neonatal Renal Tumors
Renal tumors = 7–12% ของ neonatal malignancies; มัก present เป็น palpable abdominal mass หรือ prenatal US
Congenital Mesoblastic Nephroma: ที่พบบ่อยที่สุด; ส่วนใหญ่ diagnosed < 3 months; male:female 1.5:1; รักษา: radical nephrectomy — curative stage I/II; survival > 95%

Wilms Tumor: อันดับสอง; equal sex; สัมพันธ์กับ syndromes: Beckwith-Wiedemann, WAGR, Denys-Drash, Perlman; radical nephrectomy → risk stratification → chemo/radiation; survival 90%; 16% secondary tumors ใน long-term survivors
Malignant Rhabdoid Tumor: rare, aggressive; neonatal survival 16%
Treatment principle: radical nephrectomy เป็น first-line ทุก unilateral renal tumor; biopsy first สำหรับ bilateral disease / metastases
2.5 Renal Vein Thrombosis
Risk factors: umbilical vein catheterization, perinatal asphyxia, maternal diabetes, dehydration, prothrombotic states
Classic triad: gross hematuria + palpable flank mass + thrombocytopenia
Diagnosis: Doppler US (enlarged echogenic kidneys, absent renal vein flow / increased renal artery resistance); gold standard = contrast angiography
Treatment: anticoagulation (adult ranges used); thrombolysis ใน life-threatening cases; > 60% ของ affected kidneys สุดท้ายเป็น atrophic
3. Anomalies of the Ureters
3.1 Ureteral Duplication
Incidence: 0.8–1.67%; complete = 2 ureters แยกจัน; partial = bifid pelvis รวมเป็น single ureter ที่ distal
Weigert-Meyer law: upper pole ureter → opens caudal and medial กว่า lower pole ureter (prone to ectopia + ureterocele); lower pole ureter → opens cranial and lateral (prone to VUR)
50% asymptomatic; associated: 25% upper pole obstruction, 10% lower pole scarring, 4% lower pole VUR
3.2 Ureterocele
Cystic dilatation of distal submucosal/intravesical ureter → obstruction; incidence 1 in 500; cause: failure of apoptosis of Chwalle membrane
US: hydronephrosis + cystic structure within bladder; detected prenatal as early as 16 weeks
Symptoms: UTI, urinary retention, prolapse through urethra in female → bulging vulvar mass
Management: observation (minimal VUR, no infection, no function); transurethral incision/puncture (minimally invasive, risk secondary VUR); hemi/nephrectomy สำหรับ nonfunctioning units
3.3 Ureteropelvic Junction Obstruction (UPJO)
Incidence: 1 in 1,500 live births; most common cause of prenatal hydronephrosis (41%)
Cause: newborns → intrinsic narrowing; older children → extrinsic compression by accessory vessel
Diagnosis of functional obstruction: diuretic renogram or MR urography
Intervention: 23–51% จะต้อง surgery; pyeloplasty (open/laparoscopic/robotic) success 90–100%; patients still at risk hypertension + proteinuria
50% ของ UPJO มี other urologic anomalies (contralateral obstruction, VUR) → routine VCUG recommended
3.4 Vesicoureteral Reflux (VUR)
Retrograde urine flow bladder → ureter; primary VUR = abnormal/immature ureterovesical junction; prevalence: ถึง 17% ของเด็ก; 31–36% ของเด็กที่มี UTI
Prenatal hydronephrosis: 20% จะ found to have reflux; VCUG สำหรับ high-grade hydronephrosis หรือ bilateral

rows: Observe; antibiotic prophylaxis if grade III–V or febrile UTI

blocks.text: ความเสี่ยง UTI ลดด้วย: circumcision หรือ steroid cream (physiologic phimosis); RIVUR trial NEJM 2014: antibiotic prophylaxis ลด recurrent febrile UTI แต่ benefit ต่อ renal scarring ยังไม่ชัด
4. Bladder and Urethral Anomalies
4.1 Bladder Exstrophy
Incidence: 1 in 30,000–50,000; male:female 4:1; cause: persistence of cloacal membrane → prevents mesodermal fusion → rupture → exstrophic condition
Prenatal US: (1) absent/nonfilling bladder; (2) abdominal wall mass; (3) normal kidneys + low-set umbilicus; (4) widened pubic diastasis; (5) smaller penis with anteriorly displaced scrotum

★ Immediate Newborn Management ของ Bladder Exstrophy
1. Ligate umbilical cord with silk suture (ไม่ใช่ plastic/metal clamp — prevent trauma to exposed bladder); 2. Cover exposed bladder with transparent film / hydrated gel / plastic wrap; irrigate with saline each diaper change; replace dressing daily; 3. Renal US + pelvic X-ray (pubic diastasis); sacral dimple → spinal US (tethered cord); 4. Repair delayed to 3 months (allows team coordination, testosterone surge → penile growth, bonding).

Surgical goal: renal function preservation + urinary continence + cosmetically acceptable genitalia; complete primary repair includes: bladder closure + bladder neck reconstruction + dorsal chordee repair + epispadias repair + bilateral iliac osteotomies
Outcome: ~40% achieve volitional voiding after 1 repair; 40% need additional bladder neck reconstruction at 4–5 yr; small bladders → augmentation + CIC
4.2 Cloacal Exstrophy / OEIS Complex
OEIS: Omphalocele, Exstrophy, Imperforate Anus, Spinal defects; hindgut open to abdominal wall; separates hemibladder plates

Management: neurologic eval (myelomeningocele); neonatal closure omphalocele + intestinal diversion; approximation of bladder halves; delayed exstrophy closure with iliac osteotomies; survival now close to 100%
4.3 Patent Urachus
Urachus: connects urogenital sinus → allantois; obliterated before birth normally. Patent urachus → clear fluid from umbilicus; VCUG/sinogram confirms
80% resolve spontaneously → avoid surgery < 1 yr; malignant transformation (rare) → remove if symptomatic
4.4 Posterior Urethral Valves (PUV)
Most common bladder outlet obstruction in males; incidence 1 in 4,000 live births; ONLY in males
Types: Type 1 = majority (leaflets from verumontanum fusing midline); Type 2 probably does not exist; Type 3 = annular ring (urogenital membrane persistence)
Prenatal US: thickened dilated bladder + bilateral hydroureter + oligohydramnios + dilated posterior urethra = ‘keyhole sign’
Confirmed by: VCUG postnatally — shows obstructing membranes clearly
Consequences: pulmonary hypoplasia + Potter facies (if oligohydramnios), renal dysplasia, progressive renal failure, bladder with poor compliance → myogenic failure, polyuria + nephrogenic DI

PUV: Nadir Creatinine Predicts Renal Fate
Nadir creatinine level = ตัวพยากรณ์ที่ดีที่สุดสำหรับ long-term renal function. Nadir Cr > 1.0 mg/dL → high risk ของ future renal damage. Long-term prognosis: 20–60% progress to ESRD. บิดเบา: catheter balloon inflation ใน valve bladder → ureteral obstruction — ห้าม inflate catheter balloon. Management: urinary catheter for drainage → endoscopic valve ablation เมื่อ stable; vesicostomy สำหรับ urethra เล็กเกินไป (scope เข้าไม่ได้).
Fetal interventions (vesicoamniotic shunting, fetoscopic valve ablation): ลด oligohydramnios + perinatal mortality แต่ NO evidence ลด ESRD rate
5. Genital Abnormalities in Males
5.1 Cryptorchidism (Undescended Testicle)
Incidence: 1–4.5% term; 30–45% preterm; spontaneous descent ใน 3–6 months → incidence at 1 yr: 0.8–1.2%
Etiology: multifactorial; abnormal gubernacular attachment/migration; environmental (phthalates, pesticides, flame retardants); part of ‘testicular dysgenesis syndrome’

★ DSD Alert: Bilateral Nonpalpable Testes = Evaluate for CAH
Phenotypically male infant ที่มี bilateral nonpalpable gonads → เสมอ evaluate DSD เช่น CAH — Failure to do so may cause Addisonian crisis from salt-losing adrenogenital syndrome. Hypospadias + unilateral or bilateral undescended testicles → also evaluate DSD: mixed gonadal dysgenesis or ovotesticular DSD. ความชุก chromosomal anomalies 22% ใน hypospadias + cryptorchidism.
AUA guideline (2014): NO routine ultrasound (poor sensitivity/specificity); ระบุว่า refer to pediatric urologist ที่ 6 months (ปรับ corrected GA) ถ้า testis ไม่ลง
Orchiopexy timing: 6–18 months; spermatogonia per tubule ลดลงถ้าเกิน 1 yr; surgery ช่วยเรื่อง fertility (reduce but not prevent); cancer risk ลดลงบ้าง (Hadziselimovic Lancet 2001)
5.2 Hydrocele
Persistent fluid in tunica vaginalis adjacent to testicle; occurs in > 50% of newborn males; communicating (patent processus vaginalis) → fluctuates in size
90% communicating hydroceles resolve spontaneously by 12 months; surgery (open or laparoscopic) ถ้ายังไม่หายหรือมี hernia ร่วม
5.3 Hypospadias and Chordee
Incidence: 1 in 250–300 male births (0.3%); urethral opening proximal to glans tip; spectrum: chordee + hooded foreskin + penoscrotal transposition
Classification: distal shaft / midshaft / proximal shaft / penoscrotal / scrotal / perineal; GMS score (glans, meatus, shaft) — 1–4 per component → standardizes severity
Associated anomalies: cryptorchidism in 3% (distal) to 10% (proximal); DSD if hypospadias + unilateral or bilateral undescended testes → workup for DSD
Surgery: 6–18 months; 2-stage repair สำหรับ severe hypospadias + chordee; testosterone injections → ชัดเจนถ้า micropenis; complications: 12–17% distal, 50–60% proximal ต้องกลับ revisionary surgery
5.4 Micropenis
SPL (Stretched Penile Length) measured by pressing suprapubic fat pad down → stretch penis → measure pubic ramus → glans tip; incidence: 1.5 in 10,000 male births
5.5 Other Penile Anomalies
Phimosis: tight prepuce; allow spontaneous separation; forcible retraction contraindicated — ทำให้เกิด scarring → pathologic phimosis
Webbed penis: penoscrotal fusion; ไม่ต้อง circumcise ใน newborn — refer specialist > 6 months สำหรับ foreskin repair
Buried/hidden penis: penis hidden below skin surface (excess prepubic fat / poor anchoring); palpate true length through skin; most resolve with growth and puberty

Epispadias: failure of urethral plate tubularization on dorsum; incidence 1 in 117,000 males; usually associated with bladder exstrophy; isolated: repair delayed 3–6 months; proximal (penopubic) → incontinence → needs bladder neck reconstruction + osteotomies

Testicular torsion (neonatal): extravaginal; may be synchronous or asynchronous bilateral; management controversial — most urologists advocate immediate surgical exploration (viability only assessed surgically; fix contralateral testicle to prevent anorchia)
6. Female Genital Anomalies
6.1 Embryology

Mullerian (paramesonephric) ducts form at 6–8 weeks; migrate medially + fuse + incorporate into urogenital sinus → uterovaginal canal by week 10; upper 4/5 vagina = Mullerian origin; lower 1/5 = urogenital sinus
External genitalia differentiate 12–16 weeks: genital tubercle → clitoris; urethral folds → labia minora; genital swellings → labia majora
6.2 Hydrocolpos and Hydrometrocolpos
Vaginal distension from fluid (cervical mucus stimulated by maternal hormones) อุดตันเพราะ vaginal outlet obstruction
Associated: urogenital sinus, cloacal anomalies, vaginal atresia (McKusick-Kaufman, Bardet-Biedl syndrome)
Main complication: bladder compression → urinary retention → hydronephrosis; risk of sepsis from urine collection in vaginal vault (มีรายงานถึงขั้น)
Management: imperforate hymen → incision + drainage; vaginal atresia/cloacal → CIC หรือ transabdominal vaginostomy (preferred over transvaginal — prevent reaccumulation)
6.3 Vaginal Agenesis (MRKH)

Mayer-Rokitansky-Kuster-Hauser syndrome: failure canalization of vaginal plate; 1 in 5,000 female births; most present adolescence with amenorrhea + normal secondary sex features; ovaries + external genitalia normal; uterus rudimentary
Type I: isolated ureterovaginal differences; Type II (MURCS): + renal agenesis + skeletal + ear/cardiac malformations
6.4 Cloacal Anomaly and Urogenital Sinus
Cloacal anomaly: one opening in perineum; confluence of vagina + rectum + urethra into common channel; incidence 1 in 35,000–50,000
Urogenital sinus: müllerian development arrest in first trimester; 2 openings (perineal + anal); vagina distended → hydrocolpos → manage with CIC
Management cloacal: colostomy (stool divergence) + exam under anesthesia → reconstruction at 6–8 months; teams: pediatric surgery + urology + gynecology
6.5 Introital Masses — DDx
Differential includes: imperforate hymen, prolapsed ureterocele, urethral prolapse, Skene/Gartner duct cysts, rhabdomyosarcoma (rare but serious — อย่าลืม)
7. Special Syndromes ที่ Neonatologist ต้องรู้
7.1 Prune-Belly Syndrome (Eagle-Barrett Syndrome)
Incidence: 1 in 40,000 births; 95% male
Triad: (1) abdominal wall muscle deficiency (wrinkled skin); (2) bilateral cryptorchidism; (3) urinary tract dilatation (severe hydroureteronephrosis + bladder distension + urethral dilatation)
Prognosis: ตั้งแต่ death in utero จนถึง near-normal life expectancy; 40% develop CKD; all males: bilateral orchiopexy < 1 yr; vesicostomy สำหรับ incomplete bladder emptying + recurrent UTI
7.2 Myelodysplasia (Spina Bifida, Myelomeningocele)
Incidence: 36 per 100,000 live births (US); เกิดจาก neural tube fail to close (folic acid deficiency)
Urologic goal: prevent upper tract deterioration (hydronephrosis → renal damage from elevated bladder pressure)
Workup: renal US ทุกคน + urodynamic studies → risk stratify: high risk (high-pressure bladder, high leak point, dyssynergia) vs low risk (normal compliance, low leak point)
Management: CIC ทุก 3–4 ชั่วโมง; in utero closure (MOMS trial): ลด shunting + ปรับปรุง motor outcomes; fetoscopic approach: ผล urodynamic function ดีกว่า open prenatal surgery
7.3 VACTERL Association
VACTERL: Vertebral, Anorectal, Cardiac, Tracheal, Esophageal, Renal and/or Radial, Limb anomalies; present if ≥ 3 systems
Urologic anomalies ผล — hydronephrosis + VUR บ่อยที่สุด; workup: renal US + VCUG (ถ้าผิดปกติ); sacral X-ray + spinal US (สอง tethered cord)
8. NICU Practical — GU Anomalies ที่ต้องรู้ทันที
Prenatal hydronephrosis → postnatal workup: unilateral → US ใน 1st week; bilateral or solitary kidney → US ก่อน discharge. VCUG ใน high-grade hydronephrosis
PUV (male): ใส่ urinary catheter ห้าม inflate balloon; ส่ง VCUG เมื่อ stable; ablate valves endoscopically; ติดตาม nadir Cr
Bilateral nonpalpable testes: evaluate CAH ทุกราย — electrolytes, 17-OHP, karyotype; อย่าสมมติว่าเป็น bilateral cryptorchidism แล้วรอ
Bladder exstrophy: silk suture สำหรับ cord; wrap exposed bladder; irrigate saline; renal US + pelvic X-ray; repair at 3 months แจ้งครอบครัวให้เข้าใจ
Hydrocolpos (female): สังเกต abdominal mass + single perineal opening → นึกถึง cloacal anomaly; CIC เพื่อลด urine accumulation; attention sepsis risk
ARPKD at birth: bilateral hyperechoic kidneys + oligohydramnios → respiratory support; 20% die from pulmonary complications; survivors → ติดตาม BP + renal function + portal hypertension
Adrenal hemorrhage: 2 in 1,000 live births; risk: vaginal delivery, macrosomia, perinatal hypoxia, sepsis; serial US (สอง fetal neuroblastoma ที่นำตัว mimics); supportive
Prune-belly: wrinkled abdomen + bilateral undescended testes + no apparent distress → US kidneys; vesicostomy ถ้า incomplete voiding; orchiopexy < 1 yr ทุกราย
9. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Bilateral Nonpalpable Testes = CAH จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่
Phenotypically male infant with bilateral nonpalpable gonads → evaluate for CAH immediately. Failure to do so can cause salt-losing Addisonian crisis. ส่ง: 17-OHP, electrolytes, karyotype, pelvic US. ชวน genetics + urology + endocrinology. DSD evaluation รวมถึง: hypospadias + undescended testes (unilateral or bilateral) → DSD ด้วย.

PEARL 2 — PUV: อย่า Inflate Catheter Balloon
PUV bladder มี ureteral obstruction risk จาก catheter balloon inflation. Use straight catheter / foley without inflating balloon. Confirm diagnosis with VCUG. Ablate valves endoscopically when stable. Nadir creatinine > 1.0 mg/dL = high risk ESRD — เตรียม family สำหรับ long-term renal monitoring. Fetal interventions ช่วยแค่ oligohydramnios — ไม่ได้ป้องกัน ESRD.

PEARL 3 — Bladder Exstrophy: ใช้ Silk ไม่ใช้ Plastic Clamp
Immediate care ของ bladder exstrophy: silk suture สำหรับ umbilical cord ligation (avoid plastic/metal — traumatize exposed bladder). Wrap bladder transparent film or plastic wrap; irrigate with saline each diaper change. Repair delayed to 3 months (แผน complete primary repair: bladder closure + bladder neck + epispadias + iliac osteotomies). Prenatal counseling reduces abortion rates เมื่อ consult specialist ที่มีประสบการณ์.

PEARL 4 — VCUG ทำเมื่อไหร่? ไม่ใช่ทุกราย
VCUG indications (neonates): (1) High-grade hydronephrosis บน postnatal US; (2) Bilateral hydronephrosis; (3) MCDK: เฉพาะถ้ามี contralateral anomaly; (4) Documented UTI และ US abnormal; (5) UPJO: generally recommended (50% มี associated VUR). ไม่ทำ routine VCUG สำหรับ isolated MCDK.

PEARL 5 — AUA Says No Ultrasound for Cryptorchidism
AUA 2014 guideline: ultrasound ไม่ recommend สำหรับ evaluation of undescended testes — sensitivity/specificity ต่ำ, ไม่เปลี่ยน management. Instead: refer to pediatric urologist at ~6 months (corrected GA) ถ้า testis ไม่ลง. Orchiopexy: 6–18 months. ป๊ระเด็น: ultrasound บ่อยครั้งเป็น normal practice แต่ guideline ไม่ support — จำไว้เพื่อ board exam.
10. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Peters CA et al. J Urol 2010;184:1134 (AUA VUR Guideline): Summary of AUA guideline on primary VUR management — antibiotic prophylaxis grade III–V; VCUG for high-grade prenatal hydronephrosis; conservative management first-line.

RIVUR Trial Investigators (Hoberman A et al.) NEJM 2014;370:2367: Antibiotic prophylaxis (trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole) for VUR — reduces recurrent febrile UTI but benefit for renal scarring not established; NNT to prevent 1 febrile UTI = 8.
Kolon TF et al. J Urol 2014;192:337 (AUA Cryptorchidism Guideline): Refer at 6 months if no descent; orchiopexy 6–18 months; no ultrasound; bilateral nonpalpable → evaluate DSD/CAH.
Hadziselimovic F, Herzog B. Lancet 2001;358:1156: Early orchidopexy + germ cell maturation is critical for fertility — orchiopexy should be performed early (< 1 yr) to preserve spermatogonia.
Schwab CW et al. J Urol 2002;168:2594: 15-year perspective on spontaneous VUR resolution — grades I–III resolve at 13%/yr; grades IV–V at 5%/yr; supports conservative management for lower grades.

Tanaka ST et al. J Pediatr Urol 2021;17:726 (UMPIRE study): Urodynamic characteristics of neurogenic bladder in myelomeningocele — defines 'bladder hostility' (high pressure) that predicts upper tract deterioration; supports early urodynamics + CIC.

Bilgutay AN et al. J Pediatr Urol 2016;12:179: PUV risk factors for progression to renal failure — nadir creatinine > 1.0 mg/dL is the key cutoff; other factors: bilateral hydronephrosis at diagnosis, vesicostomy requirement.

Gooskens SL et al. Pediatr Blood Cancer 2017;64:e26437: Congenital mesoblastic nephroma 50 years after recognition — staging, chemotherapy indications; overall excellent survival ~95%; cellular variant more aggressive.

Wong KF et al. J Clin Oncol 2016;34:1772 (British Childhood Cancer Survivor Study): Long-term adverse outcomes up to 50 years after Wilms tumor — cardiac disease, adverse pregnancy outcomes, renal dysfunction, secondary tumors in 16%.

Ehrlich P et al. Ann Surg 2017;266:470 (AREN0534): First prospective multi-institutional study in bilateral Wilms tumor — partial nephrectomy in appropriately selected patients is safe; disease outcomes comparable to total nephrectomy.
Balasundaram M et al. J Perinatol 2018;38:658: MCDK predictors of poor neonatal outcomes — isolated MCDK rarely requires nephrectomy; contralateral or extrarenal anomalies predict poor outcomes.

Matsell DG et al. Pediatr Nephrol 2021: Renal agenesis vs MCDK: renal agenesis solitary kidney has worse long-term renal outcomes (50% dialysis by age 30) compared to MCDK solitary kidney — different management pathways needed.

Society for Fetal Urology Consensus (Nguyen HT et al.) J Pediatr Urol 2010;6:212: Evaluation and management of antenatal hydronephrosis — standardized postnatal workup; unilateral → US at 1 week; bilateral/solitary → US before discharge; high-grade → VCUG.
11. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Male newborn มี prenatal hydronephrosis bilateral ควรทำ postnatal workup อย่างไร? เมื่อไหร่ทำ VCUG?

ตอบ: Bilateral prenatal hydronephrosis: US ก่อน discharge จาก hospital (Society for Fetal Urology consensus). ถ้า unilateral: US ใน 1st week of life. VCUG indication: high-grade hydronephrosis บน postnatal US หรือ bilateral (VCUG screen for VUR). แบบฟอร์ม: ถ้า VCUG หาก PUV suspects: VCUG shows keyhole sign. ห้ามลืม: VCUG ทำ เมื่อ stable, ไม่ทำ routinely ทุกราย hydronephrosis.
Q: PUV: prenatal signs คืออะไร? immediate management หลังคลอดทำอะไรก่อน? อะไรห้ามทำ?

ตอบ: Prenatal signs: thickened dilated bladder + bilateral hydroureter + oligohydramnios + dilated posterior urethra = ‘keyhole sign’. Postnatal management: (1) insert straight urinary catheter for drainage; (2) ห้าม inflate catheter balloon (causes ureteral obstruction); (3) VCUG เมื่อ stable → ยืนยัน diagnosis; (4) endoscopic valve ablation; vesicostomy สำหรับ urethra เล็ก. Prognosis marker: nadir creatinine > 1.0 mg/dL = high risk ESRD (20–60%).
Q: ARPKD vs ADPKD: แยกด้วย genetics, presentation, management อย่างไร?

ตอบ: ARPKD: PKHD1 gene (chr 6), AR, 1:20,000; bilateral hyperechoic kidneys + poor corticomedullary differentiation + oligohydramnios + Potter facies + pulmonary hypoplasia; liver disease (biliary dysgenesis + portal fibrosis); 20% die pulmonary; 50% need early renal replacement. ADPKD: PKD1/PKD2, AD, 1:1,000; asymmetric bilateral cysts (progressive); often asymptomatic in childhood; 20% have hypertension/proteinuria; renal failure typically adulthood. Management ADPKD: avoid routine US/cyst counting in asymptomatic; monitor BP + UA; tolvaptan for severe neonatal ADPKD.
Q: Male newborn bilateral nonpalpable testes: ควรทำอะไรทันที? เพราะเหตุใด?

ตอบ: ต้อง evaluate for DSD/CAH ทันที: ส่ง 17-OHP, electrolytes, glucose, karyotype, pelvic US. เหตุผล: phenotypically-appearing male with bilateral nonpalpable gonads อาจเป็น 46XX female with CAH (salt-losing form) — failure to diagnose → Addisonian crisis. อย่าสมมติว่าเป็น bilateral cryptorchidism แล้วรอ. Hypospadias + undescended testes (unilateral or bilateral) → DSD evaluation เช่นกัน.
Q: Congenital mesoblastic nephroma vs Wilms tumor: แยกอย่างไร? รักษาต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Congenital mesoblastic nephroma: most common neonatal renal tumor; diagnosed < 3 months; male predominance 1.5:1; treatment: radical nephrectomy — curative for stage I/II; survival ~95%; chemo rarely needed. Wilms tumor: second most common; equal sex; associated with Beckwith-Wiedemann, WAGR, Denys-Drash syndromes; radical nephrectomy → risk stratification → chemo + radiation; survival 90%; 16% secondary tumors long-term. Key: both start with nephrectomy; biopsy first สำหรับ bilateral disease / metastases.
Q: VCUG indications in neonates: list ข้อบ่งชี้ให้ครบ

ตอบ: VCUG indications: (1) high-grade hydronephrosis บน postnatal US; (2) bilateral hydronephrosis; (3) UPJO — generally recommended (50% มี VUR); (4) UTI + US abnormal; (5) MCDK เฉพาะถ้า contralateral kidney มี anomaly. ไม่ทำ: isolated MCDK with normal contralateral kidney; mild unilateral hydronephrosis (grade I–II) without infection.
Q: Horseshoe kidney: incidence, associations, complications, screening recommendations?

ตอบ: Incidence: 1 in 600 บน autopsy; male predominance; associations: Edwards, Down, Turner syndrome; urologic anomalies in 50% (VUR + UPJO). Complications: Wilms tumor ชุกขึ้น กว่า general population; ~1/3 develop stones in adulthood. Routine screening ไม่ justified เนื่องจาก risk ไม่มากพอ. Most patients asymptomatic.
Q: Prune-belly syndrome: triad คืออะไร? management goals?

ตอบ: Triad (Eagle-Barrett syndrome): (1) abdominal wall muscle deficiency (wrinkled skin); (2) bilateral cryptorchidism; (3) urinary tract dilatation (hydroureteronephrosis + bladder distension); 95% male; incidence 1:40,000. Management goals: (1) bilateral orchiopexy < 1 yr; (2) vesicostomy ถ้า incomplete bladder emptying / recurrent UTI; (3) urinary tract reconstruction ถ้าจำเป็น; 40% develop CKD — ติดตาม renal function ตลอด.
Q: Bladder exstrophy: prenatal US signs คืออะไร? immediate management ที่ห้องคลอด?

ตอบ: Prenatal US signs: (1) absent/nonfilling bladder; (2) abdominal wall mass; (3) normal kidneys + low umbilicus; (4) widened pubic diastasis; (5) smaller penis + anteriorly displaced scrotum. Immediate management: silk suture สำหรับ cord ligation; wrap exposed bladder transparent film; saline irrigation; renal US + pelvic X-ray; sacral dimple → spinal US. Repair delayed to 3 months (complete primary repair under experienced team).
Q: Cloacal anomaly vs urogenital sinus: แยกด้วยอะไร? management เหมือนกันหรือต่างกัน?

ตอบ: Urogenital sinus: müllerian arrest in first trimester; 2 perineal openings (perineal + anal); vagina confluences into common channel; anus normally located but sometimes anteriorly displaced. Cloacal anomaly: 1 perineal opening only (confluence of vagina + rectum + urethra); incidence 1:35,000–50,000. Management difference: cloacal → colostomy first + reconstruction at 6–8 months; urogenital sinus → CIC หรือ vaginostomy + later repair; both require multidisciplinary team (surgery + urology + gynecology).
Day 77 Preview

Avery Chapter 77 — Acute Kidney Injury
พรุ่งนี้: Acute Kidney Injury (AKI) — หลังจากเรียน developmental abnormalities วันนี้ เราจะเข้าเนื้อหา AKI ใน NICU: neonatal AKI definitions (KDIGO/nKIDS criteria), pre-renal vs intrinsic vs obstructive, serum creatinine รับมาจากแม่ (physiologic fall), novel biomarkers (NGAL, cystatin C), fluid management, vasopressor, aminoglycoside nephrotoxicity, และ neonatal RRT (peritoneal dialysis ใน NICU). ต่อยอดจาก Ch75 (developmental abnormalities kidneys) + Ch76 (GU anomalies) เป็น Ch77 (AKI).
